assumptions: The declared intragroup dividend has created a recognised foreign-currency-denominated intercompany receivable/payable, ie a monetary item.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of a recognised intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Once the dividend receivable is recognised, the question most directly maps to hedging foreign currency risk on a recognised item, subject to the consolidation exception for intragroup monetary items.

approaches.label: No hedge accounting because the intragroup item is ineligible in consolidation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 sets a default rule that intragroup items are not eligible hedged items in consolidated financial statements unless the specific foreign currency exceptions are met.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: If designation is considered before the receivable exists, the relevant alternative treatment is a cash flow hedge of a forecast intragroup transaction whose foreign currency risk affects consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 identifies a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk on net investments, which helps delimit cases that should not be analysed as ordinary intragroup receivable hedges.
